cards.back: A linked list is a data structure in which elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations, but are linked together through pointers.
A singly linked list is a type of linked list where each element only points to the next element in the list.
A self-referential structure is a data structure where an element contains a reference to itself, allowing it to access its own components or members.
Common operations performed on linked lists include insertion, deletion, and traversal.

cards.front: Types of Traversal in Linked Lists
cards.back: There are three types of traversal in linked lists: forward traversal, backward traversal, and depth-first traversal.
